Watson Drug Co.. Granite bloek \
Frank W. Healy, in advance for tn
Niagara Falls company. is here.
You can get anything you want for
. sewing machine At J. p. Eastman
q Co., as Riverside. Phone Main
74s. Machines rented or-sold on sasy
terms. Expert repairing.
Dave L. Fotheringham has return
ed from a trip to Butte.


Easy Work Soap-The largest cake
on the market for cents. No fancy
wrapper-all soap.


Charles s. Fee, general passenger
agent of the Northern Pacific, pass
ed through the city last night, en-
route to st. Paul from the coast.


New fall hat at Wilson's, 713 Riv-
erside avenue. ........ ....


s. NJ. Holcomb of Fort Branch
Ind, arrived from the east this morn
ing to meet his brothers. o. r. and
c. L. Holcomb Of Ritzville.


See our stock of trusses, crutches,
batteries. electric belts and shoulder
braces before buying. Watson Drug
Co., corner Riverside and Washing
ton.


The insurance companies have in
creased the rates on local department
stores about one-third. The Crescent
store, which had been paying on a
$40 rate. has been raised to $2.25;
the Palace store from $3.50 to 32.40;
the Wonder store from 31.65 to $40.
and the Whitehouse Dry Goods COM
pany from $1.65 to $2.55.
For a perfect fitting shirt see Will
son, 713 Riverside avenue.


II you become sick or meet with
any accident, you will be glad to have
something to tide you over such an
emergency. Now is the time to open
an account with the Spokane & East
ern Trust company.


The plant and machinery Of the
Inland Empire Milling company, own
ed by the Washington Water Power
company, is about to be sold to a
Holland syndicate on a basis of s40,-
600. The Hollanders have organized
the Spokane FIGURING Mills company
to operate the plant.


Stop that headache with the Right
Headache Tablet.


F. H. Newell, head Of the national
bureau in charge Of the irrigation
Of the arid west under the new ma
tional irrigation act, addressed the
chamber of commerce yesterday af-
ternoon on the irrigation of the Big
Bend country. Mr. Newell purposes
using the waters from the Spokane
and Columbia rivers.


Fail shirtIngs open Tor inspection
at Wilson's, 713 Riverside avenue.


The Club Cafe always in the lead
Our 25c merchants lunch with beer
or wine from 1870 to 2 p. m. as good
as ever. Headquarters for steaks,
chops, etc. I. Chilberg, manager.


Leake's Spokane Pocket Guide will
be out about September 10. A more
useful book has never been issued
in the city. Complete and condensed
is the information it contains, to-
gether with complete railroad and
street car time tables. map Of city
and street railways. Price, Is cents
each, $3.50 per year. John w. Ora
ham, distributors. On sale at all
news stands.
